Disable testSnapshotWithLargeSegmentFiles in S3BlobStoreRepositoryTimeoutTests (#146388)

Aggressive client settings and zero retries make this flaky on a real HTTP stack under load. S3BlobStoreRepositoryTests already covers the same scenario with representative settings.

Closes #146156

issue_title: Migrated async search index is never cleaned up
issue_body:
When the ".async-search" system index is migrated via the migration plugin, it will grow without automatic cleanup for async searches with `?keep_on_completion=true`, even once their keep_alive has expired.

This is due to how the migration plugin works. It creates a new index, named something like: ".async-search-reindexed-for-9", and then fronts the index with an alias called ".async-search". However, the cleanup task only schedules itself when the primary shard is local:
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/a414f3d06c7ab59a5a0b350e80e5674bf9864688/x-pack/plugin/core/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/core/async/AsyncTaskMaintenanceService.java#L146-L153
But there is no routing table for `".async-search"` - it's just an alias. Thus, the cleanup task is never scheduled.
commit_message:
Async Search: Ensure cleanup is also done against aliases (#146356)

The current implementation only ran cleanups if the `.async-search` index was
an index, but not if it is an alias, which could happen due to migration.

This looks up the index in case an alias is being used.

Closes #146184

commit_message:
Fix DataStreamIndexSettingsProviderTests setting count (#146395)

TIME_SERIES_USE_SYNTHETIC_ID_DEFAULT_PROD (9_093_0_00) and
TIME_SERIES_DISABLE_SEQUENCE_NUMBERS_DEFAULT (9_094_0_00) have different
version thresholds. maybeAdjustIndexSettingCount was incorrectly
assuming both settings are added together, causing off-by-one failures
when the random index version falls between the two thresholds.

Track expectedSyntheticId independently and increment the count
separately for each setting.

Closes #146126 Closes #146108 Closes #146109 Closes #146112 Closes
#146197 Closes #146198 Closes #146305 Closes #146307

Assisted by Claude

issue_title: ESQL: circular ref when excluding views
issue_body:
With the csv data sets loaded, this works:
```
FROM *,-employees_extra | LIMIT 1
```
But this complains about circular references:
```
FROM *,-employees* | LIMIT 1

circular view reference 'country_addresses': country_addresses -> airports_mp_filtered -> country_airports -> country_languages
```

The circular reference report also seems wrong, as it starts with `country_addresses` and ends in `country_languages`.

Cc @craigtaverner
commit_message:
Add csv-spec tests for views false circular reference bug (#146664)

The original fix to false circular reference errors was in the PR at
https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/146144. However, the issue
at #146208 included another edge case. In this scenario the initial fix
just changed the error from the false-circular error to an error about
invalid index name `_all`. This PR includes a wider range of tests for
both the old and new error, and a fix to the new error.

Assisted by Claude Code

Fixes #146208

commit_message:
Fix DataStreamIndexSettingsProviderTests setting count (#146395)

TIME_SERIES_USE_SYNTHETIC_ID_DEFAULT_PROD (9_093_0_00) and
TIME_SERIES_DISABLE_SEQUENCE_NUMBERS_DEFAULT (9_094_0_00) have different
version thresholds. maybeAdjustIndexSettingCount was incorrectly
assuming both settings are added together, causing off-by-one failures
when the random index version falls between the two thresholds.

Track expectedSyntheticId independently and increment the count
separately for each setting.

Closes #146126 Closes #146108 Closes #146109 Closes #146112 Closes
#146197 Closes #146198 Closes #146305 Closes #146307

Assisted by Claude

commit_message:
ESQL:DS: fix single file splitting and bzip2 bytes counting (#146373)

This fixes a bzip2 decompressor issue counting bytes with an int, instead of long.
It also update the resolution of a single file so that split discovery isn't skipped.

Closes #146330

🤖-assisted development.

issue_title: ESQL: _index LIKE with `?` in pattern doesn't work
issue_body:
Reproducer:
```
FROM k8s-downsampled METADATA _index
| WHERE _index LIKE "k8s?downsampled"
| STATS c = COUNT(*);

c:long
27
;
```

A cursory investigation with @ivancea has shown that `IndexFieldMapper` overrides `wildcardLilkeQuery` and [internally uses](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/blob/43546651315a7d9411d83f26fdbc68eeede94d42/server/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/index/mapper/IndexFieldMapper.java#L133) `Regex.simpleMatch`. The latter doesn't seem to respect `?`, only `*`.

Cc @julian-elastic
commit_message:
ES|QL: fix _index LIKE with ? wildcard (#147462)

* ES|QL: fix _index LIKE with ? wildcard

wildcardLikeQuery used Regex.simpleMatch which only handles *,
so ? was treated as a literal character. Replaced with Lucene's
WildcardQuery.toAutomaton which handles both.

Closes #146364

* Update docs/changelog/147462.yaml

* [TEST] ES|QL: _index LIKE with ? wildcard

* ES|QL: gate _index LIKE ? tests on capability

* ES|QL: return 400 for complex _index LIKE

* [TEST] ES|QL: enable _index LIKE ? CSV test

* [TEST] ES|QL: fix _index LIKE too-complex YAML test

Create a dedicated index, use METADATA _index, and clean up
after the test so it doesn't depend on indices from other tests.
Also enables the capabilities test_runner_feature in setup.

Closes #146364

commit_message:
ESQL: Allow `driver iterations` in test (#146996)

In the `testForceSleepsProfile` test we assert that there are sleeps and
stuff, but we were accidentally asserting that drivers never paused due
to iterations about 1% of the time. If everything flows faster than
expected we'll get `driver iterations` sleeps as well.

Closes #146377

Fix DateRangeDocValuesReader to appendNull for empty positions (#147153)

* Fix DateRangeDocValuesReader to appendNull for empty positions

When advanceExact returns true but decodeLongRanges yields an empty
list, no position was appended to the block builder, producing a block
with fewer positions than expected and silently triggering the
IllegalStateException guard in ValuesSourceReaderOperator.sanityCheckBlock.

Fix by calling builder.appendNull() when the decoded ranges list is
empty. Also fix TestBlock.LongRangeBuilder.appendNull(), which threw
UnsupportedOperationException and would have prevented the existing
no-doc-values null path from being tested via TestBlock.factory().

Closes #146380

commit_message:
Fix pooled SearchHits leak in MergeResult#writeTo on transport version mismatch (#146431)

This fixes pooled SearchHits leaks when MergeResult re-serializes DelayableWriteable.Serialized aggs for a different TransportVersion by expanding once and releasing top_hits hits after the write. It also handles the pre-batched branch only for the serialized expand case (referencing live aggs unchanged). Tests reproduce the refcount issue via a capturing InternalAggregations reader.

Closes #146420

issue_body:
### Description

This is to investigate uncompressing speedup: the block-boundaries aware implementation seems to pass through a file twice: once to find the block boundaries, to produce the slices, and then again block-by-block. The first pass seems also rather slow, with per byte bit fiddling, plus single-threaded. Which is also non-cancelable. 

We might also need to implement here a fallback mechanism that trades in memory for speed.
commit_message:
ESQL:DS: bzip2: implement a parallel search for block markers (#146813)

This change makes the bzip2 plugin search for the block markers in parallel. The threshold for doing so is an input of over 10MB. It makes use of ESQL's executor.
This also adds a small optimisation unrolling the per-bit-step applied to every byte.

Closes #146469

issue_title: Fix broken Lucene/Snowball documentation links in docs/ (.md → .html)
issue_body:
## Related to https://github.com/elastic/docs-content/issues/5912

## Summary

52+ files in `docs/` contain broken external links to Lucene Javadocs and Snowball stemmer pages. The links incorrectly use `.md` extensions instead of `.html`.

## Affected areas

### Text analysis (~38 files)
`docs/reference/text-analysis/analysis-*.md` — each has 1+ Lucene Javadoc links ending in `.md`

Examples of broken link targets:
- `EdgeNGramTokenFilter.md`
- `TrimFilter.md`
- `NGramTokenFilter.md`

### Similarity settings
`docs/reference/elasticsearch/index-settings/similarity.md` — ~20+ broken Lucene links:
- `DFRSimilarity.md`
- `BasicModelG.md`
- `IBSimilarity.md`
- `LMDirichletSimilarity.md`

### Query DSL (~5 files)
- `regexp-syntax.md`
- `query-dsl-shape-query.md`
- `query-dsl-geo-shape-query.md`
- `query-dsl-multi-term-rewrite.md`
- `query-dsl-mlt-query.md`

### Stemmer token filter
`analysis-stemmer-tokenfilter.md` — ~15 broken links across:
- Lucene: `ArabicStemmer.md`, `BrazilianStemmer.md`, `SoraniStemmer.md`, etc.
- Snowball (`snowballstem.org`): `armenian/stemmer.md`, `basque/stemmer.md`, etc.
- Lucene `analyzers-common`: `EstonianStemmer.md`

## Fix

Replace `.md` extensions with `.html` in all external Lucene Javadoc and Snowball stemmer URLs throughout the affected files.

## Notes

This was identified via automated link checking across the `docs/` directory. The total number of broken links across 52+ files is significant enough to warrant a bulk fix.

commit_message:
ESQL: Fix COUNT+query push down losing aggs (#146555)

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/146479

For a query like `STATS COUNT(*), MAX(ROUND_TO(...)) by y | DROP y`, the `PushCountQueryAndTagsToSource` rule tries to push it down based on:
- There's a single COUNT
- There's a BY
- There's a Lucene Query (RoundTo generates it)

However, the DROP removes the grouping from the aggs, and a missing condition thiks the second agg is a grouping, effectively discarding it.

Made with AI assistance

commit_message:
[ESQL] Return null blocks for range fields with doc_values disabled (#147602)

* [ESQL] Return null blocks for range fields with doc_values disabled

RangeFieldMapper.blockLoader() now returns ConstantNull when doc_values
is disabled, instead of throwing IllegalStateException. Removes the
GenerativeRandomMappingRestTest workaround that suppressed this error.

Closes #146527

* [ESQL] Add unit and integration tests for range field no-doc-values

Add testNoDocValuesBlockLoader to IntegerRangeFieldMapperTests and
testBlockLoaderWithDocValues to verify the new blockLoader behavior.
Add testDateRangeNoDocValues to FieldExtractorTestCase as an end-to-end
REST test confirming null values are returned instead of an error.

* [ESQL] Bump DATE_RANGE capability to V3 to guard no-doc-values fix

DATE_RANGE_FIELD_TYPE_V2 was already present in the bwc snapshot before
the no-doc-values null-return fix landed, so mixed-cluster tests could
proceed past the capability check and then fail on old nodes that still
throw IllegalStateException. Renaming to V3 ensures the capability is
absent from old nodes, causing the test to be skipped until the whole
cluster is upgraded.
